episodes.title: The Dark Side of 1970s American Cults Nobody Talks About | Boring History for Sleep
episodes.description:
Hey guys! We’re now live on Spotify 🎧
Listen here: https://open.spotify.com/show/3K57vHeztR0HKUbKeXtEnx

The 1970s are often remembered for freedom, self-discovery, and spiritual exploration but beneath the surface, a darker reality was unfolding. Across America, cults were quietly growing in power, control, and influence, often with devastating consequences for those involved.

In this video, we uncover the dark side of 1970s American cults that rarely gets discussed. From psychological manipulation and isolation tactics to financial exploitation and abuse, many groups operated behind a façade of peace and enlightenment. As trust in traditional institutions declined, vulnerable individuals were drawn into tightly controlled communities that were difficult to escape.

Resources:
Cults in America: A Reference Handbook – James R. Lewis – https://www.abc-clio.com/products/a3867c/

Jonestown and the Peoples Temple – FBI Archives – https://vault.fbi.gov/jonestown

The 1970s Cult Phenomenon – History.com – https://www.history.com/topics/crime/cults

Understanding Cults and Psychological Manipulation – American Psychological Association – https://www.apa.org/monitor/nov02/cults
